The '''Deep Space Manufacturing Facility''', otherwise known as the '''X7 Factory''' or '''X-7 factory station''', was a spaceborne manufacturing plant of standardized design produced by both [[Kost & Ko Factories]] and the [[Telgorn Corporation/Legends|Telgorn Corporation]].

==Use==
Crew quarters and labs topped the station's central spire, from which emanated four modules for storage and additional lab space.<ref name="CSWE" />

==Defenses==
Due to the high value of many of these products, the X7's designers saw fit to equip it with a significant armament in order to deter raiders. A total of eight [[Turbolaser/Legends|turbolaser]] turrets defended the station from almost every direction, plus a warhead launcher typically loaded with eight [[Proton torpedo/Legends|proton torpedoes]] or ten [[Concussion missile/Legends|concussion missiles]] was able to discourage starfighter attack.

X7 Factories were equipped with an anti-warhead defensive system, which launched missiles to take out incoming ordnance. It took a few salvos of enemy missiles to overwhelm such a system. This system was so successful that some crucial [[XQ Platform series|XQ Platforms]] were fitted with it as well, such as [[Demetrius Zaarin]]'s [[TIE/d "Defender" Multi-Role Starfighter/Legends|TIE Defender]] research facility in the [[Parmel system/Legends|Parmel system]].

When destroyed, the X7's four modules would split up from the central hub, in a similar manner to the XQ Platform's landing pads/docking arms.

==History==
During [[Admiral/Legends|Admiral]] [[Demetrius Zaarin|Zaarin's]] short-lived insurrection, when his coup against Emperor [[Palpatine/Legends|Palpatine]] failed, his strategy shifted to the destruction of X7 Factories located in the [[Omar system/Legends|Omar system]]. These were involved in the manufacture of the [[TIE/ad starfighter|TIE Avenger]] starfighter. The renegade Imperial officer used a mixture of [[TIE/IN interceptor/Legends|TIE/IN interceptors]], [[TIE/sa tactical bomber/Legends|TIE/sa bombers]], and [[Gamma-class ATR-6 assault transport|assault transports]], supported by a [[EF76 Nebulon-B escort frigate/Legends|EF76 Nebulon-B escort frigate]] (according to the cutscene, though it was a [[Nebulon-B2 frigate]] in the mission). Zaarin's strike caught the loyalists off guard, fortunately loyalist Imperials led by [[Maarek Stele/Legends|Maarek Stele]] managed to evacuate the personnel and inflicted significant losses to the attackers before the three Avenger facilities were destroyed.

''[[ZA-13]]'' which manufactured the Avenger's hyperdrive was successfully recaptured from Zaarin's control. With equipment delivered by a supply convoy, it was converted to full Avenger production. However, it was soon afterwards destroyed by a strike from the traitor Admiral. This killed off any hope of widespread adoption of the TIE Avenger.<ref name="Moby cover">{{MobyGames|url=game/4634/star-wars-tie-fighter-defender-of-the-empire/cover/group-4902/cover-8228|text=Star Wars: TIE Fighter - Defender of the Empire cover art}}</ref>

[[Ali Tarrak]] had her [[Nami]] species construct a facility to manufacture TIE Defenders. It was well protected by a minefield of [[Mine Type C|Type Cs]] and Defender starfighters. From his flagship ''[[Grey Wolf]]'', Admiral [[Mitth'raw'nuruodo/Legends|Thrawn]] launched a two-pronged assault on Tarrak's facility. A lone [[Missile Boat]] piloted by [[Maarek Stele/Legends|Maarek Stele]] was instrumental in taking out the mines, Defenders, and the station's anti-warhead launcher, allowing [[TIE/sa tactical bomber/Legends|TIE/sa bombers]] to destroy the factory itself.

==Behind the scenes==
*The Kost & Ko company is referenced in the tech-room stats for both [[Star Wars: TIE Fighter (video game)|''TIE Fighter'']] and [[Star Wars: X-Wing vs. TIE Fighter|''X-wing vs. TIE Fighter'']] as the producer of the X7 Factory. However, in [[Star Wars: X-Wing Alliance|''X-wing Alliance'']], the manufacturer was listed as the Telgorn Corporation.

*While ''Star Wars: TIE Fighter'' had pioneered the XQ Platform space station, the X7 Factory was not available until the ''Defender of the Empire'' expansion. For [[Maarek Stele#Service under Thrawn|Tour of Duty 3 Mission 1]], a combination of cargo containers was used to represent the abandoned manufacturing facility at [[Argoon]].
